title: Tabletop RPGs: General Knowledge
description: Explore systems, terminology, and history across tabletop role-playing games in this 30-question challenge.

questions.text: What does the abbreviation TTRPG stand for?
questions.explanation: TTRPG stands for Tabletop Role-Playing Game.

questions.text: What is the primary role of a GM or DM in most TTRPGs?
options: Present the world, adjudicate rules, and portray NPCs
questions.explanation: The GM/DM facilitates play by presenting situations, running NPCs, and adjudicating outcomes.

questions.text: What does NPC usually mean in role-playing games?
questions.explanation: NPC means Non-Player Character, typically controlled by the GM.

questions.text: In many games, what does 'session zero' usually refer to?
options: A pre-campaign planning and expectations session
questions.explanation: Session zero is commonly used to align group expectations, tone, character concepts, and safety tools.

questions.text: Which die is commonly abbreviated as d20?
questions.explanation: A d20 is a twenty-sided die.

questions.text: What is a 'critical hit' in many RPG systems?
questions.explanation: A critical hit usually represents an unusually strong successful attack or action.

questions.text: What does 'homebrew' usually mean in a TTRPG context?
options: Custom content made by the group or GM
questions.explanation: Homebrew refers to custom rules, settings, monsters, classes, or adventures created by players or GMs.

questions.text: What is a campaign in tabletop RPGs?
options: A long-running linked series of sessions
questions.explanation: A campaign is an ongoing series of adventures connected by characters, setting, and story.

questions.text: What is a one-shot?
options: A game intended for one session
questions.explanation: A one-shot is designed to be completed in a single session.

questions.text: Which of these is best known as a science-fiction TTRPG?
questions.explanation: Traveller is one of the most well-known science-fiction tabletop RPGs.

questions.text: Call of Cthulhu is most associated with what style of play?
questions.explanation: Call of Cthulhu is known for investigative horror inspired by H. P. Lovecraft's cosmic themes.

questions.text: What does 'actual play' typically refer to?
options: Recorded or streamed sessions of people playing a TTRPG
questions.explanation: Actual play refers to watching or listening to real RPG sessions, often as streams, videos, or podcasts.

questions.text: What is a common purpose of safety tools in RPGs?
options: Helping players set boundaries and comfort levels
questions.explanation: Safety tools help groups communicate boundaries and maintain a respectful, comfortable play environment.

questions.text: What is meant by 'rules-lite' in RPG design?
questions.explanation: Rules-lite systems use fewer and simpler mechanics to keep focus on fast play and narrative flow.

questions.text: What does 'crunchy' usually imply about an RPG?
questions.explanation: Crunchy games have more detailed mechanics, options, and often denser rules interactions.

questions.text: In many RPGs, what is initiative used for?
options: Set turn order, especially in combat
questions.explanation: Initiative commonly determines the order in which participants act during structured encounters.

questions.text: What is metagaming?
options: Using player knowledge a character would not reasonably have
questions.explanation: Metagaming often means using out-of-character knowledge to influence in-character decisions.

questions.text: Which game is strongly associated with the Storyteller system and modern gothic horror themes?
questions.explanation: Vampire: The Masquerade is a flagship Storyteller-system game known for personal and political horror.

questions.text: What does OSR generally stand for in RPG discussions?
questions.explanation: OSR commonly means Old School Revival/Renaissance, a movement inspired by early RPG play styles and design.

questions.text: Powered by the Apocalypse (PbtA) games are inspired by which title?
questions.explanation: PbtA games derive from Apocalypse World's move-based design framework.

questions.text: Fate Core is especially known for which narrative mechanic?
questions.explanation: Fate emphasizes Aspects and Fate points to drive story-relevant character traits and scene details.

questions.text: What does RAW typically stand for?
questions.explanation: RAW means Rules As Written, referring to strict interpretation of published text.

questions.text: What does RAI typically stand for?
questions.explanation: RAI means Rules As Intended, focusing on likely design intent rather than literal wording alone.

questions.text: What is the purpose of a character sheet?
options: To summarize a character's stats, abilities, and key info
questions.explanation: A character sheet tracks game-relevant character data such as attributes, skills, gear, and advancement.

questions.text: What is an adventure module?
options: A prewritten scenario for a game system
questions.explanation: An adventure module is a prepared scenario that a GM can run, often with maps, NPCs, and plot hooks.

questions.text: What is a common benefit of virtual tabletops (VTTs)?
options: They remove the need for any GM
They support online play tools like maps, tokens, and dice
They only work for one game system
questions.explanation: VTTs help remote groups play by providing shared maps, character tools, and digital dice systems.

questions.text: What does 'theater of the mind' usually describe?
options: Resolving scenes through spoken description rather than tactical maps
questions.explanation: Theater of the mind relies on narrative description and imagination instead of precise map grids.

questions.text: GURPS is notable for being what kind of system?
questions.explanation: GURPS stands for Generic Universal RolePlaying System and is designed to support many genres.

questions.text: What is one hallmark of many indie TTRPG designs?
options: Single mandatory setting for all games
Narrowly focused themes and mechanics for specific experiences
questions.explanation: Many indie RPGs focus tightly on a specific tone, theme, or style, often with purpose-built mechanics.

questions.text: What is the shared goal of most tabletop RPG groups?
options: To always win by rules lawyering
To collaboratively create engaging experiences and stories
questions.explanation: Most groups aim for collaborative fun through shared storytelling, challenge, and memorable moments.
